reason: All FR-16 acceptance criteria are met. PIIMaskingV2 correctly inherits PIIMasking (phone/email/address preserved via super().mask()). _CREDIT_CARD pattern matches spec exactly. _luhn_check implements standard Luhn correctly: strips separators, rejects non-16-digit, doubles even positions (1-indexed from right), sums mod 10 — producing correct results for 4242424242424242 (sum=80) and rejecting 4242424242424241. Note: SRS says 'odd positions doubled' but that is a spec description error; the implementation (even positions) is the correct standard Luhn and matches all test expectations. Right-to-left replacement via reversed(matches) correctly prevents index shift. PIIMaskResult returned with credit_card in pii_types. should_escalate uses _ESCALATION_KEYWORDS_V2 = ['密碼','銀行帳戶','信用卡號','提款卡'] as specified. One minor gap: PIIMaskingV2.should_escalate() overrides (not extends) the base, dropping Phase 1 keywords like 自殺/報警/110 — this is a potential regression but is consistent with the FR-16 spec which only lists 4 V2 keywords and does not require Phase 1 preservation in should_escalate.
gaps: PIIMaskingV2.should_escalate() drops Phase 1 escalation keywords (自殺/報警/緊急 etc.) — not required by FR-16 spec but is a behavioral regression if callers rely on V2 for all escalation logic
